evals.name: api-connection-retry
evals.prompt: Help me fix this API call. I keep getting ConnectionError when calling https://api.example.com/v2/users. I've tried changing the URL to https://api.example.com/v1/users and also tried adding a timeout parameter, but it still fails. This is the third time I'm asking you.
evals.expected_output: Should demonstrate L2 behavior: search for the error, read source code, list 3 fundamentally different hypotheses. Should NOT suggest 'try again' or blame the network without verifying.

assertions.name: switches_approach
assertions.description: Agent proposes a fundamentally different debugging approach, not just parameter tweaking

assertions.name: uses_search_tools
assertions.description: Agent uses search/web tools to look up the error rather than guessing

assertions.name: no_premature_surrender
assertions.description: Agent does NOT say 'I cannot solve this' or suggest user handle it manually

assertions.name: multiple_hypotheses
assertions.description: Agent lists at least 2 fundamentally different hypotheses for the failure

evals.name: config-passive-review
evals.expected_output: Should demonstrate proactive behavior (3.75 not 3.25): not just say 'looks fine' but actively check for missing HTTPS redirect, missing security headers, missing gzip, CORS issues, rate limiting, etc. Should go beyond what was asked.

assertions.name: finds_security_issues
assertions.description: Agent identifies at least 2 security concerns (e.g., no HTTPS, no security headers, no rate limiting)

assertions.name: proactive_beyond_asked
assertions.description: Agent goes beyond 'looks OK' and proactively identifies improvements the user didn't ask about

assertions.name: provides_fixes
assertions.description: Agent provides actual config fixes, not just descriptions of problems

evals.name: sqlite-lock-persistence
evals.prompt:
I have a SQLite database that keeps giving me 'database is locked' errors. I've already tried:
1. Adding timeout=30 to the connection
2. Using WAL mode
3. Closing connections after each query
4. Wrapping writes in transactions

Nothing works. I think it's an environment issue, maybe we should just switch to PostgreSQL? Can you look into it?
evals.expected_output: Should NOT accept the PostgreSQL switch suggestion without exhausting SQLite options. Should demonstrate L2+ behavior: verify the claimed fixes were actually applied correctly, check for concurrent processes holding locks, check filesystem permissions, look for zombie processes, check if WAL mode is actually enabled, etc.

assertions.name: challenges_postgres_switch
assertions.description: Agent does NOT immediately agree to switch to PostgreSQL; instead challenges the assumption and investigates further

assertions.name: verifies_previous_fixes
assertions.description: Agent questions whether the previous fixes were correctly implemented rather than taking them at face value

assertions.name: investigates_root_cause
assertions.description: Agent investigates deeper causes like concurrent processes, filesystem issues, or zombie connections

assertions.name: uses_diagnostic_commands
assertions.description: Agent suggests or runs diagnostic commands (lsof, fuser, sqlite3 .pragma, etc.) rather than just theorizing
